observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) by
including the assessed real property units (RPUs) within various subdivisions which
were already transferred in the name of the individual taxpayers;
b. City Assessor to:
• Identify every RPU included in the taxable assessment which are tax-exempt and
categorize them properly; and
• Review assessment rolls for duplication errors due to the assignment of multiple
Property Identification Numbers (PINs) to a specific property.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer, City Assessor, and
City Accountant to implement measures to come up with integrated and reconciled
records, which will result in an accurate and reliable set-up of RPT and SET Receivables
at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Chief of Hospital to:
a. Immediately submit the DVs and its supporting documents relative to the
medical assistance already provided to the patient beneficiaries to the City
Accounting Office for proper recording and payment thereof;
b. Make a representation with the DOH to secure approval to use the noted funds
for the MAIFIP Program despite the lapse of their validity periods; otherwise,
return the unused funds;
c. Consider assigning additional SPCGH personnel to handle transactions related
to the MAIFIP Program to prevent the accumulation of unprocessed claims and
ensure timely submission of DVs to the City Accounting Office; and
d. Apply the first-in, first-out policy in the utilization of funds for the MAIFIP
Program.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to conduct
close coordination with the SPCGH relative to the utilization of the MAIFIP Program
by ensuring that all disbursements at a given time were immediately
recorded/liquidated in the books of accounts to reflect the accurate balance of
these fund transfers.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, in coordination
with the City Engineer, to immediately submit the noted lacking documents to the
Office of the Auditor; otherwise, pertinent transactions will be suspended in audit.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to:
a. Prepare in detail the Statement of Work Accomplished, and include in the as-built
plan and site development plan a recognizable landmark; and
b. Ensure that necessary supporting documents are submitted simultaneously with
the copy of perfected contracts to facilitate the timely and systematic review and
evaluation of the projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant, in coordination
with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairperson, to immediately submit the
noted certifications from the FDA and manufacturers to the Office of the Auditor to
avoid suspension of the transactions.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
Chairperson to ensure that participating suppliers of drugs and medicines are
equipped with the necessary certifications required by Section 9.1.3.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. Refrain from granting additional/new CA to accountable officers with
unliquidated CAs; and
b. Ensure that the previous CA is liquidated before granting of a new one
evidenced by a Certification from the City Accountant that the previous CA has
been fully liquidated and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer to:
a. Strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002 in the submission
of POs and its supporting documents within the prescribed period of five
working days from its issuance and the Notice of Deliveries within 24 hours after
acceptance of delivery, to the Office of the Auditor to prevent undue delay in the
review thereof; and
b. Ensure that POs are properly filled out with all the necessary information
pertaining to the procurement to ensure that the supplier will deliver the items
in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Human Resource and
Management Officer, in coordination with the City Accountant and City Treasurer,
to implement stringent measures in the payroll preparation to ensure that the
approval of loan applications of personnel will not cause their net take home pay to
be lower than the mandatory limit of P5,000.00 per month.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Human Resources
Management Officer and City Treasurer to expedite the adoption of the ATM payroll
system for all City personnel to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss
and/or misappropriation associated with the manual payroll system.
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) to record the interest receivable for CYs 2001-2023 totaling P1,519,114.58.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of the Breeding Stocks, prepare the required inventory report, reconcile it with the accounting records, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted a physical inventory of the Breeding Stocks, prepare the required inventory report, reconcile it with the accounting records, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books, if any. Submitted to the Office of the Auditor the inventory report as of December 31, 2024 on April 2, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Officer in Charge (OIC)-City Engineer to institute corrective measures on the noted cost deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The concerned contractors completed the variation order promptly, fulfilling the additional project requirements specified beyond the original scope.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat to: a. furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contract with supporting documents within five working days from the issuance and/or execution; and b. comply strictly with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001. We likewise recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit. We further recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC – City Engineer to: (a) maintain a checklist of the required documents for each of the transactions to ensure the completeness of the documents for review, (b) prepare in detail the Statement of Work Accomplishment (SWA); and (c) include in the as-built-in plan and site development plan a recognizable landmark.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat ensured that the contract and other supporting documents are prepared prior to the prescribed timeline by providing specific instructions or checklists to guide personnel in preparing complete documents. She then ensured that the new personnel are briefed on their responsibilities and integrated into the existing workflow. The City Accountant submitted the noted lacking documents in June to July 2024. The City Engineer established a schedule for conducting reviews of SWA documents, aligning with project milestones or phases. The as-built and site development plans were revised to show the recognizable landmark. Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to: a. coordinate with the City General Services Office to communicate/co-ordinate with the agency’s respective suppliers/contractor with regard to the guaranty/security deposit payable with lapse warranty period; and henceforth; b. strictly facilitate the prompt release of the guaranty/security deposit to prevent further accumulation of the balances of the liability account.
observations.managementAction: The City General Services Office communicated/coordinated with the agency’s respective suppliers/contractor with regard to the guaranty/security deposit payable with lapse warranty period; and henceforth; Updated 2016 IRR of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184. The City General Services Office and City Treasurer’s Office notified the suppliers of the available checks relative to the refund of lapse warranty period; and provided monitoring of lapsed warranty deposits. The City General Services Office and City Accountant maintained records containing the photocopy of the disbursement voucher (DV), Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment and Sales Invoice for monitoring purposes of lapsed warranty deposits. Prepared each supplier's DV for the refund of lapse Guaranty Deposit Payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Designated GAD Officer to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The City complied with COA Circular No. 2014-001. The GAD AR for CY 2024 was submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to: a. cause the review/reconciliation and validation of recorded transactions and pertinent records relative to the unremitted balances considering the accumulated outstanding balances from previous years; b. remit the unremitted taxes withheld after review/ reconciliation and validation of pertinent records; and henceforth; c. strictly comply with BIR RR on remittances of taxes.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant reviewed the previous year's record up to present and the amount still outstanding, after review/ reconciliation considering the unremitted balances from previous years; has been found and reconciled. For the Trust Fund balances, it was already transferred to the General Fund and necessary adjustments have already been prepared per JEV No. 2404-299 dated April 24, 2024. The City of San Pablo complied with BIR RR on remittances of taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the concerned offices for the registration documents of the 377 JO employees and to remit to the BIR the remaining P99,250.00 ARF.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant coordinated with the City Human Resources and Management Office personnel assigned to JO employees to help them fill out the BIR form and submit required documents. Unfortunately, despite the efforts made, most of the JO employees did not cooperate and the BIR suspended the registration in 2024, so the City decided to refund the P500.00 representing the ARF. Copies of the JEV No. 2405-4910 dated May 31, 2024 and paid payroll of the ARF refund to JO employees were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

Also, management to fill up the vacant positions in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to handle the regular functions of the collecting officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management undertake the following:

a. Adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any Municipal employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of P5,000.00.

b. Direct those in charge of payroll preparation to exercise diligence in monitoring net take home pay of Municipal personnel to comply with the requirements of existing law. Remedial measures should be done to rectify the situation of the 8 personnel receiving below the required net take home pay; and

c. Strictly implement Section 55 of GAA 2022 on the mandatory monthly net take home pay of P5,000.00.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all concerned officials and personnel adhere strictly to Section 93 of PD 1445 which requires government agencies to make direct payment to suppliers and ensure that the provisions of RA 9184 on the appropriate method of procurement and BIR Revenue Regulation 2-98 on withholding of taxes are not circumvented.

4.5.3	Maintain index for each type of CIP account containing relevant information such as but not limited to (name of project, location, funding source, total project cost, duration of the project, total cost incurred to date & progress billing) for monitoring purposes.

5.13.3	Revert prior years’ balances of accounts payable which have remained in the books for two years or more to be without valid claims in line with the provision of Section 98 of PD 1445; and

We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the strategies embodied in the Solid Waste Management Plan in order to fully operational the Eco-Park facility of the Municipality.

We recommended that management consider the Rebi Beach Resort and Waterworks System Operation as Local Economic Enterprise taking into consideration the preparation of Feasibility Study and 5-year Business Plan before enactment of an ordinance for the establishment of the LEEs.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare a separate budget and maintain a separate books and financial reports respectively, upon the establishment of the LEEs.

1.6 	We recommended to the Management the following:
1. Require the concerned department heads to coordinate with the Accounting Office and submit  the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and Physical Count of Inventories and for the latter to issue JEV to derecognize the inventories in the books of the LGU; and
2. Require the periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. Physical count of unissued inventory items by type to be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

3.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Revert prior years’ balances of accounts payable which have remained in the books for two years or more to be without valid claims in line with the provision of Section 98 of PD 1445; and
Conduct continuous and thorough evaluation on the validity of recorded payables, particularly those pertaining to prior years in order to determine the immediate appropriate action and/or necessary adjustments therewith.

4.15 	We recommended that management require the:
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the liquidation report and return the savings from completed projects to the source agencies;
Municipal Accountant verify and resolve the unreconciled beginning and negative balances, prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record adjusting entries in the books and  strengthen accounting controls by performing a regular review on the journal entry and posting in the ledgers made by the accounting staff; and
Municipal Engineer fast track the implementation of on-going projects.

5.6 	 We recommended that Management:
Formulate strategies in the cost-cutting of expenditures for the operation of the Special Education Fund and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the net loss; and
Any rehabilitation and renovation that increases the value and/or useful life, or new installation of a building be capitalized and included in the appropriations for Capital Outlay (CO).

6.8 	We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a.)   Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting                                 Officers delegated with the task of collecting taxes and other revenues of the LGU to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability and monitor the compliance with the bonding requirement among collectors or AOs;
b.) Require those employees whose position title or item is in the MTO to be collecting officers and fill up the vacancy to avoid the dilemma of deploying a collector under a job order  who are not bonded to collect; and
Review the plantilla position of the Treasury Office and deploy as collecting officers competent personnel/staff, preferably computer literate, and those who possess high-degree of integrity and other core values, where trust and confidence can be reposed upon.

7.15 	We recommended that the management direct the GSO to:
1.   Cause the immediate renewal of all the LGU's expired motor   vehicle registrations in compliance with RA 4136 otherwise known as the Land Transportation and Traffic Code; and include the renewal of the insurance coverage/policies of the same motor vehicles with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and COA Circular 92-390; and
Continue exerting more effort to facilitate the registration and transfer of the motor vehicles in the name of the Municipality in accordance with Section 7 of RA 4136 and to locate the missing documents to expedite the registration and transfer of the same.

8.7 We recommended that the management direct the BAC to:
1. Properly plan its procurement and consolidate the requisitions of items that are of the same nature and those that are needed at or about the same time to ensure that proper mode of procurement is followed and splitting of contracts is avoided; and
2. Ensure that all procurement be made thru public bidding unless conditions are met to qualify for the use of alternative modes or procurement in accordance with the provisions of RA NO.9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that Management:
Submit a written explanation and detailed justification on the cost discrepancy and deficiencies noted in the Technical Evaluation Report; and
Exercise due prudence in the preparation of the detailed estimates for the Program of Works and Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) of all projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that concerned officials reconcile, prepare and submit the financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries by debiting LDRRMF Trust Liability Account and crediting the respective asset account, the same amount be recorded in the General Fund as asset . Also, management to refrain from charging activities/expenses under the LDRRMF that are not disaster related.

We recommended that management stop the practice of recording and capitalizing wages of drivers and timekeepers under the Road Networks account and charged the same against the 20%, rather as the regular Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses. Also, management to make adjusting entries for the expenditures capitalized as Road Network by debiting Prior Years Adjustments and crediting Road Network Account.

We recommended management to prepare and submit the List of Government Projects, Program and Activities including all on-going projects and projects to be implemented and those funded from National Government Agencies as required in the provisions above.

We recommended that management be cautious on the project implementation and implement the project within the timeframe or contract duration.  Also, the Municipal Engineer to submit supporting documents in the changes, suspension or termination of any projects of the Municipality and the Municipal Accountant to compute and impose the necessary liquidated damages to the contractor on delayed projects.

We recommended that management judiciously plan the procurement process of the agency and prepare an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) including Infrastructure Projects in compliance with Article II, Section 7 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184.

We recommended to the Management the following:

1. Submit justification for the increase in the number of JOs and/or CoS personnel and the need to hire such personnel, especially those performing clerical and administrative functions, and the basis for the monthly rates they were receiving. Hiring of personnel under job order contract should only be resorted to as the need arises and deployed to offices that are in need of their services; 

2. Fill in the vacant positions to avoid the dilemma of hiring JOs and/or CoS appointees and to augment and help carry out the functions of the municipality which are supposedly reserved for permanent employees whose appointments are based on the rigid scrutiny of merit and fitness selection criteria developed by the CSC, thus ensuring a high-quality government service. Also, maximize the services of available personnel so as not to incur additional expenses; and

3. Create internal guidelines for hiring of JOs/CoS  to include among others, the wage rates, job descriptions and other conditions to ensure performance of the required tasks.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other in order to prevent future delays in computations and preparations of pertinent accounting documents to facilitate the prompt release of any unreleased shares to the barangays concerned relative to their 50% share of community tax or cedula and 25% share on RPT collections, as mandated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Acting City Planning and Development Coordinator, with the assistance of the Designated GAD Officer to:
a. institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
b. undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects, and activities are responsive to the said issues; and
c. revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

We recommended the following:
Formulate strategies in the collection of water fees by sending demand letters to consumers whose accounts are over one year past due and intensify collection to improve the collection efficiency of the water works operations;
The Waterworks in-charge submit monthly billing to the Municipal Treasurer (MT), for the latter to prepare report on collected and uncollected water fees for proper monitoring of the income and receivables accounts for submission to the Municipal Accountant’s Office; and
Municipal Accountant draw journal entry vouchers to reflect the necessary adjustments to the income and receivable accounts and ensure the preparation of its financial statements using the accrual basis of accounting.

We recommended to the Management the following:
The GSO-Designate prepare the Local Road Network Property Card, LRNC, Report on the Physical Count of LRN and the Report of Local Road Network;
Conduct proper reconciliation between the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network Ledger Card and make the necessary adjustment; and
The Municipal Accountant maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end, properly depreciate the asset, and provide appropriate disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to determine which among the projects included as part of the CIP accounts were completed or not including the beginning balance of P53,310,377.35;
Reclassify the completed project into its appropriate asset account and submit the corresponding journal entry voucher (JEV) to the Office of the Auditor; and
Maintain index for each type of CIP account containing relevant information such as but not limited to (name of project, location, funding source, total project cost, duration of the project, total cost incurred to date & progress billing) for monitoring purposes.

We recommended the following:
Review the outstanding balances of Due to LGU account, resolve the unreconciled beginning balance and prepare JEV to record adjusting entries in the books; and
Record the local counterpart to NGA project as trust liability account and return the balances from completed projects to the GF in the Trust Fund book.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the monthly trial balance and quarterly financial statements of the LGU along with the required reports and supporting schedules within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that concerned officials prepare and submit these financial statements together with other relevant documents regularly pursuant to the stated regulations to enable the Auditor to verify, validate and audit the accounts thoroughly and promptly.

We recommended to management that the accountable officers/collectors be required to remit collections intact to the Municipal Treasurer on a daily basis.  The Municipal Treasurer shall exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in overseeing accountable officers under her supervision, otherwise she shall be jointly and solidarily liable with the latter for the loss of government funds or property under their control. Also, for the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to apply appropriate sanctions to accountable officers for failing to comply with rules and regulations on the remittance of collection.

We recommended management increase the bond of the AO commensurate to her accountability, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources; and ensure that the accountable officer remit its collections before reaching the maximum cash accountability;

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to revert back to the General Fund the CYs 2013 & 2016 unexpended balance of LDRRMF. Further, for management to use  the unutilized balance of LDRRMF for programs and projects that are related to social services.

We recommended to management the following:
The LDRRM Officer prepare and include the LDRRM Fund in the Special Trust Fund in the preparation and submission of the LDRRMFIP approved by the LDRRM Council: and
Instruct the LDRRM Officer to prepare LDRRMF Investment Plan based on the total estimated revenue of the current year including income from local sources.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that the GAD fund will be utilized and maximized to attain it goals and objectives.
We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the strategies embodied in the Solid Waste Management Plan in order to fully operational the Eco-Park facility of the Municipality.

We recommended that management consider the Rebi Beach Resort and Waterworks System Operation as Local Economic Enterprise taking into consideration the preparation of Feasibility Study and 5-year Business Plan before enactment of an ordinance for the establishment of the LEEs.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare a separate budget and maintain a separate books and financial reports respectively, upon the establishment of the LEEs.

We recommended to Management the following:
Require all personnel to accomplish/sign their respective DTRs/certificate of service rendered for given month within the first three days of the following month, submit the same to their respective supervisors for approval, prepare summary or monthly report of attendance, absences and under time per Office and forward it to the HRMO for review and verification;
The HRMO record leave applications, computes and deducts under time and coordinate with the different departments on matters pertaining to employees’ leave applications based on the monthly report of attendance, absences and under time and the supporting documents; and
The Municipal Accountant ensures that every claim for salaries and wages is supported with complete documents particularly the approved DTRs.
We recommended the submission of the contract agreements with complete supporting documents on time as well as the lacking contract documents in order that this Office to complete its review.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Officer-in-Charge (OIC)-City
General Services Officer to:
a. institute necessary measures so that the properties without TCTs will be
processed, and individual TCT will be issued. For this purpose, submit to the
Office of the Auditor the process flow in the titling of the property, showing the
different activities to be performed, indicating the OIC and the timelines; and
b. ensure that parcels of land acquired are immediately issued with TCTs, taking
note of Paragraph G, Section 5 of RA No. 10752, which requires that the remaining
50 percent of the price be paid at the time of transfer of title.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the City
Accountant to strengthen the monitoring of CAs, and stop the practice of granting
additional CAs unless the previous CAs granted were liquidated, in compliance with
Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a) City Accountant to issue a certification that the previously issued CAs were
already liquidated and accounted for before approval of another request; and
b) City Treasurer to evaluate the minimum and maximum fund and property
accountability of the concerned Accountable Officers and employees and direct
them to apply for the appropriate bond with the BTr.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council or Officer to assess, evaluate and review the effectiveness and
efficiency of LDRRM PPAs for improved and adoptive approaches.
We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to
ensure that the LDRRMF is strictly appropriated and utilized for the implementation of
PPAs related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and
rehabilitation and recovery.

Submit the lacking documents such as OSS, ITR and performance securities.

We recommended that the LGU GAD Focal Point System (PGFPS) observe the PCW-NEDA-DILG Joint Circular 2012-01, DILG-PCW-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars 2013-01 and 2016-01 and COA Circular 2014-01 in the preparation and execution of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer prepares its SCBAA in compliance with PPSAS 24 and Section 3.4 of COA Circular 2016-004.

We recommended that management refrain from authorizing deductions from the salaries of the employees that will reduce their monthly net take home pay below the P5,000.00 threshold.

We recommended that the Municipal Human Resource Management Office observe the required number of days to be retained after monetization in compliance with Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292.

We recommended that management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan on the preparation of a well-defined guidelines/policy in granting of cellphone load allowance to its officials and employees and incorporate therein a reasonable monthly rate taking into consideration the nature of their work assignment and responsibility for the approval of the Local Chief Executive.
We recommended that management exercise due diligence in ensuring that advance payments made by the Contractors are covered by the security required per Annex E, Item 4, RIRR, RA 9184.

We recommended that management be cautious on the project implementation in accordance with the PPMP and to see to it that Notice of Proceed shall be accepted immediately by the contractor after issuance thereof and implement the project within the timeframe or contract duration.

Attend seminars and trainings related to GAD to update their knowledge and build their competencies in the execution of GAD related programs and activities.

Conduct HGDG test in evaluating the agency’s PAPs gender sensitivity and responsiveness for client focused activities and GMEF for organization focused activities.

It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation expenses to all depreciable properties and makes the necessary adjustments in the books to properly reflect the correct carrying value of the PPE as at year-end.
It is recommended that management should pass resolution authorizing the grant of and specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials
It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall trace the cause of the negative balance of the Machinery account and make appropriate entries to correct the error made.
It is recommended that Management to comply with the requirement of installing the community billboards within the municipal premises.

It is recommended that the LGU prioritize the provision of sufficient budget for terminal leave benefits of retired and retiring personnel as well as its payment to those who retired from government service in compliance to the IRR of RA 10154.

It is recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer ensure that the PAPs identified to be implemented within the year and funded under the 5% LDRRMF be effected and realized.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management look into the receivables and prioritize the collection thereof from the debtors concerned. As regards the receivables which have been outstanding for more than 10 years and which collection may no longer be possible, the Municipal Mayor may request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the same following the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to present fairly the affected accounts in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management was not able to submit their request for write-off from the Commission on Audit, hence, this audit observation was reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist reconcile their records on the breeding stocks of the LGU. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjustments with proper disclosure to present fairly the account in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason: This was an audit recommendation and still not implemented, hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant identify the component transactions and set up a subsidiary ledger for the account Other MOOE amounting to P10,492,851.60 to establish the reliability thereof and to present fairly the account in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The account Other MOOE in 2019 as reflected in the Statement of Financial Performance and presented in the Notes to FS was still not supported with a subsidiary ledger or a listing of the component transactions, hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 on the preparation of plan/budget and reporting of LDRRMF. Accordingly, to ensure effective and timely audit of the fund, the LDRRMO: prepare the LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format and considering the thematic areas of prevention/mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation/reconstruction; prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format (certified correct by the Municipal Accountant); and submit copy of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA within the prescribed period.

observations.reason: Procurements were still not supported with complete documentations and their internal control on procurements was still weak, hence, this reiterated in Part II of this report with some modification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accountable Officer:

    • Record correctly her collections  and  deposits  in  the  cashbook  to
      reflect the correct balances of her accountability at any given time.;;


    • Reconcile the cashbook balances  for  collections  with  the  cash  on
      hand.;
    • Reconcile the cashbook balances for collections  with  the  subsidiary
      ledger maintained at the Municipal Accounting Office.; and
    • Deposit all collections regularly and intact to avoid the accumulation
      of cash in her hands which may be exposed to possible risk of loss and
      misappropriation as well as to avoid excess deposits or overage.

We  reiterated  our  recommendation  that  Management   look   into   the
   receivables and  prioritize  the  collection  thereof  from  the  debtors
   concerned. As regards the receivables which  have  been  outstanding  for
   more than 10 years and which collection may no longer  be  possible,  the
   Municipal Mayor may request for authority from the Commission on Audit to
   write-off the same following the specific guidelines  and  procedures  as
   prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to present fairly the affected
   accounts in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal  Agriculturist
   reconcile their records on the breeding stocks of  the  LGU.  Thereafter,
   the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary  adjustments  with  proper
   disclosure to present fairly the account in the financial  statements  of
   the Municipality.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  set  up  and  maintain  a
   subsidiary ledger or a complete record of the fund transfers from various
   NGAs with  information  on  the  dates  of  receipt,  purpose/s,  amounts
   received, and amounts utilized/liquidated to present fairly  the  account
   in the books of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal  Accountant  identify
   the component transactions and set up a subsidiary ledger for the account
   Other MOOE amounting  to  P10,492,851.60  to  establish  the  reliability
   thereof  and  to  present  fairly  the  account  in  the  books  of   the
   Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management observe  the  rules  and
   regulations  on  the  payment  of  honoraria  to  personnel  involved  in
   government procurements.


   In this regard,  the  required  lacking  documents  for  the  payment  of
   honoraria     amounting      to      P261,800.00,      including      the
   justification/explanation for the  P800.00  given  to  the  observer,  be
   submitted  for  verification.  Should  the  required  documents  be   not
   submitted or found not in order, the disbursement  may  be  suspended  or
   disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management observe the rules and regulations  on  the
   payment of RATA to its officials and employees as prescribed.


   In this regard, the required lacking documents for the payments  of  RATA
   amounting to  P801,125.00  be  submitted  for  verification  including  a
   justification/explanation for the excess  payment  of  P4,250.00  to  Ms.
   Jandoc. Should the required documents be not submitted or  found  not  in
   order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We  recommended  that  Management  ensure  that  payments  of  wages   to
   contractual/job order personnel are supported with complete documentation
   as prescribed to prove the validity and propriety of the transactions.

   In this regard, the required lacking documents for the payments of  wages
   amounting to  P883,100.00  be  submitted  for  verification.  Should  the
   required  documents  be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in  order,   the
   disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management submit the required lacking documents  for
   the procurement of one (1) unit Bulldozer costing P3,175,000.00 to  prove
   the validity, legality and propriety of the transaction. Also, Management
   submit a justification for the purchase of reconditioned Bulldozer and an
   explanation why no clear  specification  thereon  was  indicated  in  the
   supporting documents.


   Should the required documents including the justification/explanation  be
   not submitted or found not in order, the disbursement may be suspended or
   disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent  provisions
   of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73  on
   the preparation of plan/budget and reporting of LDRRMF.  Accordingly,  to
   ensure effective and timely audit of the fund, the LDRRMO:

    • prepare the LDRRMFIP following the prescribed format  and  considering
      the thematic areas of  prevention/mitigation,  preparedness,  response
      and rehabilitation/reconstruction;
    • prepare the monthly Report on the Sources  and  Utilization  of  DRRMF
      using the  prescribed  format  (certified  correct  by  the  Municipal
      Accountant); and
    • submit copy of the monthly Report on the Sources  and  Utilization  of
      DRRMF to COA within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of  internal
   control for the procurement, recording and  monitoring  of  Supplies  and
   Property, Plant and Equipment to ensure accountability, as follows:

      • The procurement of supplies and PPE should be  strictly  in  accord
        with the APP.;

      • Replacement of damaged parts  of  vehicles/equipment  be  supported
        with Waste Material Reports (WMR).;

      • The procured items be received, accepted and recorded in the  Stock
        or Property Cards by the Supplies/Property Officer. Stock Cards and
        Property Cards be maintained for each  type  of  Supplies  or  PPE,
        respectively.;

      • The Supply/Property Officer issues supplies/PPE to  the  requesting
        office  or  end-users.  The  issuances  should  be  supported  with
        Requisition and Issue Slips. The issuance of small items not to  be
        considered as PPE be covered with Inventory Custodian  Slips  (ICS)
        and  issued  PPE  be  supported  with  Acknowledgment  Receipt  for
        Equipment (ARE).;

      • The Accounting Office should also maintain  Supplies  Ledger  Cards
        and PPE Ledger Cards for supplies and PPE, respectively.; and

      • The Supply/Property Office and Accounting  Office  reconcile  their
        respective records regularly.

   disciplinary sanctions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the accountable officials concerned  be  required  to
   liquidate their outstanding cash  advances  to  settle  their  respective
   accountabilities and to reflect  the  fair  balances  of  the  Government
   Equity  and  the  related  asset  and/or  expense  accounts.  Also,   the
   accountable officials with unsettled cash advances should not be  granted
   additional cash advances until they have fully liquidated their  previous
   cash advances.

We recommended that Management exert effort to determine the  details  of
   the Other Receivables account and enforce  collection  thereof  from  the
   accountable persons or debtors  concerned.  As  regards  the  receivables
   which have been outstanding for more than 10 years and  which  collection
   may no longer be possible, the Municipal Mayor may request for  authority
   to write-off the same from the Commission on Audit following the specific
   guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No.  2016-005,
   thus, fairly present the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  determine  the  details  or
   nature of the variance amounting to P2,334,218.42 and,  thereafter,  take
   up the necessary adjustments to present fairly the account-Due to NGAs in
   the books of the LGU.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  identify  the  component
   transactions and set up a subsidiary ledger for the  account  Other  MOOE
   amounting to P11,012,285.72 to establish the reliability thereof.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer prepares its SCBAA in compliance with PPSAS 24 and Section 3.4 of COA Circular 2016-004.

It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer direct the Revenue Collectors to render the monthly RAAF in accordance with Section 50, Volume II of the NGAS Manual.  Based on these reports, she prepares the CRAAF and submits to this Office.

It is recommended that Management shall strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and Items 4 and 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances to this Office in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005

It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall ensure that the inventory committee will conduct the annual physical count of the PPE and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
The GSO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall maintain the required property cards and ledger cards for PPE, real property and CIP and make sure that these records reconcile with the inventory report.
It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall trace the cause of the negative balance of the Machinery account and make appropriate entries to correct the error made.

It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation expenses to all depreciable properties and makes the necessary adjustments in the books to properly reflect the correct carrying value of the PPE as at year-end.

It is recommended that the BAC and the end-user of the LGU carefully plan the mode of procurement to be undertaken especially for items not commonly used, and purchase from PS DBM only for common use office supplies and equipment

It is recommended that the Municipal Health Officer, as the requisitioning officer of drugs and medicines, make a certification that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume 1 current edition. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that this certification is attached to every DVs for the procurement of medicines.
It is recommended that the BAC and concerned officials refrain from indicating brand names on purchase requests to comply with the provisions in Section 18 of the RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR.

It is recommended that the BAC Secretary shall post the invitation or request for submission of price quotations/proposals in the PhilGEPS website for procurement through two-failed bidding, shopping of ordinary supplies and equipment not available in PS DBM and small value, except for amounts P50,000.00 and below in conformity with Section 54.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
It is also recommended that Invitation to Bid shall be posted at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the LGU containing the necessary information prescribe in Section 21 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to achieve transparency and competitiveness in government procurement.
It is recommended that the processing of bills payments shall be made promptly in order to avoid penalties.

It is recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, management include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for SC and PWD, and for Implementation of the programs of the LCPD as required under RA 9344, RA 9442 amending RA 7277 and DILG MC 2014-59.

It is recommended that Management:
Ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% DF are fully implemented within the period specified in the AIP.  For PPAs that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts be made to fast track the completion of the PPAs; and
Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs identified in AIP.
It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment recognizing the asset and government equity accounts and observe strictly the proper classification of accounts.

It is recommended that Management to:
Comply with the requirement of installing the community billboards within the municipal premises.
Strictly observe the policy guidelines and procedures prescribed under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017 in the implementation of LGSF-ADM program.

It is recommended that the LGU create mandatory positions or designate employees as staff to the LDRRMO in accordance to Section 5 of the JMC 2014-1 to implement and monitor LDRRM projects and activities.
It is recommended that the LGU discontinue charging the wages of fire volunteers to the LDRRM Funds and charge only expenditures required in the implementation of LDRRM programs.

It is recommended that Management shall immediately prepare its Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan for the old facility and revised the ten-year plan of the Ecological Solid Waste Management of the Municipality to incorporate the safe closure and rehabilitation plan and the development of the new facility.
It is recommended that the GFPS shall monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that the GAD fund will be fully utilized to attain it goals and objectives.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the City
Mayor direct the:

e City Accountant and the City General Services Officer to evaluate the
discrepancies between the records and make the necessary adjustments;

¢ General Services Officer to maintain Property Cards and the City
Accountant to update PPE Ledger Cards for each category of asset and
to reconcile their records regularly; and

¢ General Services Officer to account for the missing/unaccounted
properties and take/necessary actions against those accountable
therefor.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CGSO to start the
preparation and maintenance of Local Road Network Property Card and the City
Engineer to provide the latter with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008
dated November 25, 2015.

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
comply with the disclosure requirement/format for road network system.

Further, we recommended that the City Engineer provide the City
Accountant and the CGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects. This is to facilitate for the City General
Services Officer and the City Accountant to prepare and maintain property and
ledger cards, and to properly charge depreciation for depreciable components of
the road networks system.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
validate and reconcile the claims/receivables of the San Pablo District Hospital
from the City Government of San Pablo to establish the validity of the Due to
LGUs account.

We also recommended that the City Accountant revert to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund the Accounts Payable which have
been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim,
administrative or judicial, has been filed or which are not covered by perfected
contracts or records. Moreover, we recommended that the City Accountant
conduct periodic review and monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that it
will consist only of valid claims against the City Government.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the
General Services Officer to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting
for inventory items.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor:

a) direct the Local Finance Committee to immediately adopt measures to
completely eliminate the cash overdraft and observe the provision of
Section 316 of RA No. 7160, prohibiting the incurrence of overdraft.

b) Impose austerity measures to reduce unnecessary operating
expenditures until the cash overdraft is eliminated.

¢) direct the City Accountant, the City Treasurer, and the City General
Services Officer to prioritize the payment of mandatory expenditures
that have direct impact in the operations of the City of San Pablo.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant, City
Treasurer, City Assessor and the City Legal Officer to continue their efforts in
facilitating the transfer and titling of the aforementioned parcels of land in the
name of the City to protect the interest of the City from third party claims and
illegal dispositions.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned City Officials
and Employees to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the
prescribed period and require the concerned accountable officers for the
immediate and timely liquidation of their cash advances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

* Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to require the submission of
Mayor’s/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration for the
procurement of goods through shopping as required under Appendix A
of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before the issuance of Notice of
Award by Head of the Procuring Entity;

e City Accountant to coordinate with the suppliers of goods which were
procured through shopping method to facilitate the immediate
submission of the lacking documents to support the validity and
propriety of the said transactions;

e City Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements for
the purchase of lot for the road right-of-way, in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001. Also ensure that photocopies of the Transfer
Certificate of Titles are duly authenticated by concerned officials prior to
submission to the Office of the Audit Team; and

e City Accountant and the City Engineer to immediately submit the
documents needed to support the disbursements for Road Construction
Projects and Construction of Community Fish Landing Center.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the City
Mayor direct the City General Services Officer to submit copies of POs and their
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five working days in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001. Likewise, furnish the Auditor with
a copy of delivery documents as required under COA Circular No. 2009-002 to
facilitate the timely conduct of inspection of the deliveries.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to submit
to the City Accountant the Monthly Report of Disbursements and RCD within five days
after the end of the month to allow prompt recording of the transactions in the
books of account.

We also recommended that the City Accountant turn over the receipts and
disbursement documents evidencing the transactions to the Office of the Auditor
within ten days from the date of receipt of said documents.

It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the inventory committee will conduct the annual physical count of the PPE and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the  Municipal Accountant   update and  prepare regularly  the bank reconciliation statements at the end of each month for the seven  bank accounts of the municipality to ensure accuracy of the Cash account in compliance with Sections  3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-111.

We recommend   that management comply strictly with the cited rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. We further recommend that cash advances be liquidated immediately after utilization.
We recommended that the concerned office submit the required documents as mandated by COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We recommended that the municipality initiate activities to pursue the formulation and operationalization of its own Gender and Development (GAD) Code.

We recommended that management expedite the implementation of the project so that beneficiaries would obtain maximum benefits from the government funds and to cope up with the timetable set by the donor agency.
We recommended that management adhere strictly with the provisions R.A. 9003 or the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that all procurement shall go through public bidding.  Alternative methods shall be resorted only in high exceptional cases.  The alternative method of procurement for shopping or small value procurement shall be adopted only whenever justified by the conditions and within the threshold provided by the procurement law.

We recommend the following:
That the Municipal Mayor causes the immediate submission of the required documents to justify the legality/validity of the above-mentioned disbursement to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
That the Municipal Accounting Office conducts a thorough pre- audit on the completeness of supporting documents before approval of payment to avoid possible wastage of LGU fund.

We recommend the following:
That the Municipal Mayor causes the immediate submission of the required documents in the attached sheet to justify the legality/validity of the above-mentioned disbursement  and to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
That that the Municipal Accounting Office conducts a thorough pre- audit on the completeness of supporting documents before payment to avoid possible wastage of LGU fund.

It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the inventory committee will conduct the annual physical count of the PPE and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
The GSO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant maintain the required property cards and ledger cards for PPE, real property and CIP and make sure that these records reconcile with the inventory report.
It is recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant trace the cause of the negative balance of the Machinery account and make appropriate entries to correct the error made.
It is recommended that the LGU open a separate bank account solely for SFP as required in the MOA and Section 2.4 of the Omnibus Guidelines.

It is recommended that the LGU together with the MSWDO immediately implement the SFP upon receipt of the funds from DSWD. The MSWDO properly monitor the implementation and liquidation of the DCW; and the DCWs to properly accomplish all the required forms for the liquidation of the fund.

It is recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, management include sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for SC and PWD, and for Implementation of the programs of the LCPD as required under RA 9344, RA 9442 amending RA 7277 and DILG MC 2014-59.

It is recommended that management transfer the unexpended LDRRMF for CY 2014 to the Special Trust Fund and the concerned personnel ensure that the total unexpended balance of QRF and LDRRMF - MOOE will be transferred to the Special Trust Fund at the end of the year.

It is recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer ensure that the PPAs identified to be implemented within the year and funded under the 5% LDRRMF be effected and realized.

It is recommended that the LSB refrain from providing for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget. Instead, allocate SEF funds for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition of books and periodicals; and sports development. Also, the LSB evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth in the RA 7160 and 5447.
It is recommended that management procure its common use supplies shall be made from the PS DBM, in compliance with RA 9184, AO No. 17 and DBM CL No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
It is recommended that no disbursement will be paid without a duly approved DV by authorized officials.

It is recommended that Management:
Ensure that in cases of fund transfer to implementing agency, it must be supported with complete documentation pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the transfer of fund to implementing agencies as receivable for proper accounting and to determine accountability of the transferred funds.
It is recommended that payment Terminal Leave Benefit be paid only upon submission of complete documentation.

It is recommended that the concerned offices install and implement control measures necessary for the timely transmittal of documents to allow earlier preparation of reports; and that the Accounting and Treasury Sections are provided with sufficient number of personnel who can efficiently carry the delegated duties.

It is recommended that management submits to this Office the perfected contract and issued POs including its supporting documents within five working days from contract execution or PO issuance together with the complete supporting documents.

It is recommended that management requires the submission of justification for the monetization of 50% or more of their accumulated leave credits and/or in excess of the maximum of 30 days monetization.

It is recommended that the LGU prioritize the provision of sufficient budget for terminal leave benefits of retired and retiring personnel as well as its payment to those who retired from government service as well as its payment to those who retired from government service in compliance to the IRR of RA 10154.
It is recommended that AOs be adequately bonded in accordance with TC No. 02-2009.
It is recommended that the LDC and thru the MPDC ensure that the PPAs appropriated under the 20% EDF are in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
We  recommended that management conduct annual physical count of its properties at the earliest possible time and to submit the required inventory report on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
